title: ficus peracuta
intro: Ficus peracuta, also known as the Indian laurel or wild fig, is a small, deciduous tree or shrub native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Ficus peracuta include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation and pain, and protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Ficus peracuta can be prepared into herbal decoctions, infusions, or extracts for medicinal use, although its preparation and dosage should be consulted with a healthcare professional or an experienced herbalist.
